forecasts.question: Will AGI be developed before 1 January 2030?
forecasts.key_change: Slight upward revision; Hassabis (DeepMind) gives 50% by 2030; Kalshi markets at ~40%; prediction market consensus 25-35%.
forecasts.reference_class: Expert forecasts and prediction markets on AGI timing
forecasts.base_rate_source: Samotsvety superforecasters 2023 estimate ~28%; Metaculus aggregate near 50% by mid-2030s

factors_up.factor: Rapid AI capability gains (GPT-5 class models, reasoning breakthroughs)
Industry leader bullishness: Hassabis 50%, Altman says AGI-level by 2030
OpenAI/Anthropic/Google racing with vast resources

factors_down.factor: Strict resolution criteria requires credible public announcement or 3+ top researchers
Definition ambiguity — companies may achieve capabilities without claiming AGI
Remaining ~3.75 years is short window for transformative leap

forecasts.integration: Anchor on Samotsvety base rate of ~28%, adjust slightly upward given accelerating pace; strict resolution criteria tempers enthusiasm.
key_uncertainties: What counts as 'credible' AGI announcement vs marketing claim
Whether capability gains continue at current pace or plateau
forecasts.update_triggers_up: GPT-6/Claude 5 class model passes robust AGI benchmark widely accepted by researchers
forecasts.update_triggers_down: Major AI capability slowdown; hardware shortage; safety pause imposed

forecasts.question: Will economic consultancy lose ≥33% value/jobs by end 2030 due to AI?
forecasts.key_change: Unchanged. No new evidence of sector-specific 33% decline; global AI job disruption projections remain moderate, not catastrophic.
forecasts.reference_class: Sector-level employment/revenue disruption from prior technology waves
forecasts.base_rate_source: Historical technology disruption rarely causes 33% sector loss in 6 years; Forrester: 6% of US jobs lost to AI by 2030

factors_up.factor: AI rapidly automating knowledge work and data analysis core to consultancy
41% of employers plan AI-driven workforce reductions (WEF)

factors_down.factor: 33% threshold is very high — consultancy likely to adapt/expand rather than shrink that much
Net job creation expected globally (170M new roles per WEF)
Consultancy demand often increases with complexity — AI creates new consulting needs

forecasts.integration: Base rate very low for this magnitude of disruption in this timeframe; inside view factors raise probability modestly but 33% threshold remains a high bar.
key_uncertainties: Speed at which AI agents can replace billable consultant hours
Whether 'due to AI' attribution can be established
forecasts.update_triggers_up: Major consultancy announces >20% AI-driven headcount cuts; revenue benchmarks show >15% decline
forecasts.update_triggers_down: Consultancy sector reports revenue growth despite AI; new service lines expand workforce

forecasts.question: Will Labour be largest party at next UK General Election?
forecasts.key_change: Revised down: Reform holds 7-point polling lead (March 2026); MRP modelling shows Labour potentially 3rd by seat count behind Reform and Conservatives.
forecasts.reference_class: UK governing parties recovering from deep polling deficits to win next election
forecasts.base_rate_source: Historical base rate: governing parties with ~8pp polling deficit 3+ years from election have recovered about 25% of the time, but current deficit is unusually broad-based

factors_up.factor: Election is up to Dec 2029 — 3+ years for Labour to recover
Reform vote could split right-of-centre vote, benefiting Labour in FPTP
Economic recovery could boost incumbent

factors_down.factor: Reform at 26.5% vs Labour at 18.1% — largest polling deficit for governing party in modern history
Greens at 16.6% eroding Labour's left flank simultaneously
MRP modelling shows Labour potentially 6th by seat count in one scenario

forecasts.integration: The breadth of the electoral collapse (attacked from left by Greens, right by Reform) is historically unusual; even with recovery time, the mountain is very steep.
key_uncertainties: Whether Reform can sustain polling once it faces real scrutiny as potential government
Degree of vote splitting under FPTP
forecasts.update_triggers_up: Labour returns to 25%+ in polling; Reform implodes (scandal or policy failure)
forecasts.update_triggers_down: Labour poll below 15% consistently; Reform sustains majority-winning poll numbers

forecasts.question: Will AGI be developed before 1 January 2035?
forecasts.key_change: Unchanged. Metaculus aggregate near 50% for AGI by mid-2030s; 9-year window supports moderate-to-high probability.
forecasts.reference_class: Expert and prediction market consensus on AGI by 2035
forecasts.base_rate_source: Metaculus ~50% by mid-2030; AI expert survey 50% by 2047 implies ~40-45% by 2035; Manifold users median 2035

factors_up.factor: 9-year window with current AI pace
Exponential compute scaling and algorithmic improvements
Trillion-dollar investment in AI infrastructure

factors_down.factor: Definition ambiguity — may achieve without formal AGI declaration
Possible capability plateaus or safety-driven slowdowns

forecasts.integration: Anchor on ~55% base rate; adjust upward given current acceleration and 9-year window.
key_uncertainties: Whether current scaling continues or hits fundamental limits
Whether resolution criteria (credible announcement/3+ researchers) is met even if capabilities achieved
forecasts.update_triggers_up: Consistent AGI-level benchmarks achieved and accepted by research community
forecasts.update_triggers_down: Fundamental capability ceiling identified; major resource constraints materialise

forecasts.question: Will China initiate a military blockade or invasion of Taiwan before 1 January 2030?
forecasts.key_change: Unchanged. US intelligence confirms China not planning near-term invasion; Polymarket ~10% by end 2026; risk rising but still low probability before 2030.
forecasts.base_rate_source: Polymarket ~10% by end 2026; Manifold ~37% by 2030 (likely overestimate); ASPI and US DoD assessments suggest low near-term probability

factors_up.factor: PLA capability improvements narrowing window of US intervention advantage
Taiwan's continued de facto independence creating pressure for resolution
US geopolitical distraction (Ukraine, Middle East) could reduce deterrence

factors_down.factor: US intelligence: China not planning invasion (confirmed March 2026)
Amphibious invasion assessed as extremely high-risk for PLA
Economic interdependence creates huge costs for China
4 years remaining in window — most risk concentrated in 2027-2030

forecasts.integration: Weight heavily on authoritative intelligence assessment; Manifold 37% likely overestimates risk, particularly for the strict resolution criteria (blockade >72h or assault on Taiwan proper).
key_uncertainties: Xi Jinping's timeline and risk tolerance
Taiwan 2028 election outcome and cross-strait relations
forecasts.update_triggers_up: Large-scale PLA exercises simulate blockade; Taiwan presidential election produces independence escalation
forecasts.update_triggers_down: Cross-strait dialogue resumes; US-China relations stabilise significantly

forecasts.question: Will UK annual CPI inflation fall below 2.0% for at least one month before 1 January 2027?
forecasts.key_change: MAJOR REVISION DOWN (from 33%): BoE March 2026 revised CPI forecast to 3-3.5% for Q2-Q3 2026 due to energy price shock. Current CPI 3%; return below 2% before Jan 2027 now very unlikely.
forecasts.reference_class: UK CPI falling to <2% from current 3% within 9 months against active energy price shock
forecasts.base_rate_source: BoE February forecast had CPI at 2.1% in Q2 2026; March revision to 3-3.5% eliminates that pathway

factors_up.factor: Energy prices could reverse quickly if Middle East conflict de-escalates
Base effects: last year's energy price rises will drop out

factors_down.factor: BoE explicitly revised forecast: CPI 3-3.5% through H2 2026
Energy price shock from Middle East conflict ongoing
Current CPI at 3% — needs 1pp+ drop in remaining 9 months

forecasts.integration: BoE's own revised projection is the most authoritative signal; the March 2026 revision is a decisive downward update. Only a rapid energy price reversal could change this.
key_uncertainties: Speed and sustainability of Middle East energy price impact
Whether ceasefire reduces energy prices before year-end
forecasts.update_triggers_up: Middle East ceasefire causes rapid energy price drop; ONS publishes unexpectedly low CPI print
forecasts.update_triggers_down: Energy prices escalate further; BoE further revises CPI forecast upward

forecasts.question: Will Spider-Man: Brand New Day gross over $1.5B worldwide by 31 December 2026?
forecasts.key_change: Revised up: trailer became first in history to cross 1B views; projections of $2.54B worldwide; $1.5B threshold comfortably within reach.
forecasts.reference_class: MCU Spider-Man films and Marvel tentpoles box office performance
forecasts.base_rate_source: Spider-Man: No Way Home grossed $1.9B; MCU films with comparable hype typically gross $1.5-2.5B; ~80% of MCU tentpoles exceed $1.5B

factors_up.factor: First trailer ever to cross 1 billion views — unprecedented pre-release demand
Projections of $2.54B worldwide from tracking analysts
Tom Holland, Zendaya, full MCU marketing machine
Extended theatrical window announced

factors_down.factor: Not yet released (July 31) — execution risk remains
Market saturation from Avengers: Doomsday earlier in 2026
Global economic conditions could suppress discretionary spending

forecasts.integration: Extraordinary pre-release signals (1B trailer views) warrant strong upward adjustment from base rate. $1.5B is a relatively modest target given projections of $2.54B.
key_uncertainties: Critical reception (poor reviews could hurt legs)
forecasts.update_triggers_down: Poor reviews (Rotten Tomatoes <50%); significant critical backlash

forecasts.question: Will OpenAI and Anthropic have a combined ARR exceeding $70B by 31 December 2026?
forecasts.key_change: Revised down slightly: combined run-rate currently ~$44B (OpenAI $25B + Anthropic $19B as of March 2026); needs ~60% growth to Q4 — achievable given trajectory but less certain.
forecasts.base_rate_source: Combined March 2026 run-rate $44B; Anthropic growing ~10x/year historically; OpenAI growing ~3.4x/year

factors_up.factor: Anthropic at $19B run-rate in March, growing ~10x/year historically
OpenAI's ad revenue pilot ($100M ARR in <2 months) adds new revenue stream
Enterprise AI adoption accelerating globally

factors_down.factor: Combined $44B in March needs 60% growth to $70B by Q4 — growth may decelerate
Anthropic's $70B target is for 2028, suggesting 2026 will be far lower
Resolution requires 'most recent credible reporting' — may lag actual Q4 ARR

forecasts.integration: Strong growth trajectory supports reaching $70B, but current combined of $44B means significant acceleration needed. The 60% growth in 9 months is ambitious but not implausible given Anthropic's pace.
key_uncertainties: Whether Anthropic's hyper-growth rate sustains through 2026
Timing of when Q4 2026 ARR figures get reported/verified
forecasts.update_triggers_up: Anthropic announces $30B+ ARR mid-2026; OpenAI raises 2026 guidance significantly
forecasts.update_triggers_down: Anthropic growth decelerates significantly; OpenAI revenue misses guidance

forecasts.question: Will UK average annual real GDP growth exceed 1.5% across the 2030s (2030-2039)?
forecasts.key_change: Revised down: OBR March 2026 forecast cuts 2026 growth to 1.1% (from 1.4%); sluggish near-term trajectory weakens case for sustained 1.5%+ in the 2030s.
forecasts.reference_class: UK real GDP growth averages across decades
forecasts.base_rate_source: UK 2010s averaged ~1.8% but includes Brexit shock; post-2020 recovery modest; OBR near-term forecasts 1.1-1.6% growth

factors_up.factor: AI productivity dividend could boost growth across the decade
Demographic factors may ease (net migration supporting labour supply)
Post-austerity public investment cycle may mature

factors_down.factor: OBR March 2026: 1.1% growth in 2026, 1.6% in 2027 — weak starting point
High debt (95% of GDP by early 2030s) constrains fiscal space
Sluggish productivity growth structural issue
Trade headwinds from post-Brexit frictions persist

forecasts.integration: Slight downward revision from weak near-term OBR figures; the decade-long average remains uncertain with AI upside a genuine wildcard; anchor near historical post-2010 performance.
key_uncertainties: Whether AI productivity gains materialise at scale in UK economy
Fiscal position and debt sustainability through 2030s
forecasts.update_triggers_up: UK growth surprises above 2% consistently in late 2020s; AI productivity gains evident in ONS data
forecasts.update_triggers_down: OBR long-run growth forecasts revised below 1.5%; structural debt crisis
